title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.3)
statement_text: For every forest $H$ and every integer $k\geq 1$, there exists $c\geq 1$ such that $\chi(G)\leq c$ for every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$.
context_text: The Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture is stated as a central piece of background motivation. The paper notes that if true, every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$ would have a stable set of size at least $|G|/c$; the authors' companion paper [16] proved a near version ($\alpha(G)\geq |G|^{1-o(1)}$). The current paper proves an analogue for $K_t$-subdivision-free graphs (Theorem 1.1), which the authors explicitly compare to what Gyárfás-Sumner would imply.
paper_contribution: Proves a polylogarithmic analogue for $K_t$-subdivision-free graphs: every such graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq c_t|G|/(\log|G|)^{3t-5}$, showing that the Gyárfás-Sumner phenomenon (large stable sets in $\chi$-bounded classes) extends — in a near sense — well beyond the forest-exclusion setting.
notes: Conjecture 1.3 is explicitly labelled and attributed. The paper also records a false analogue (Conjecture 1.5, attributed to Scott [18]) which is already disproved and hence not extracted as an open problem.
